behavior_summary: Handles the player's 'U8P_CANOPYTP::look' (examine) action for the canopy object. When invoked it prepares local item state (init / set info / process-exclude semantics inferred), then asks the item to 'bark' the short descriptive string "canopy ".
The return value from Item::bark is captured and propagated via an 'implies' operation, which the script uses to set or influence the resulting script outcome, then the process suspends/returns.
No global game flags are read or written by this script; behavior is local to the canopy instance and the immediate bark/display interaction.
notable_control_flow: Entry on the 'U8P_CANOPYTP::look' trigger -> local init/state setting (inferred 'set info' / 'process exclude') -> push receiver and string -> call Item::bark("canopy ") -> capture return (retval) -> apply 'implies' to propagate/convert retval into a result/flag -> pop temporary -> suspend/return.
open_questions: Precise semantics of the 'implies' opcode in this engine: does it convert a bark return into a boolean success, set a script-level result, or influence event propagation?
Exact meaning and target of the inferred 'set info' / 'process exclude' operations performed at function start: which info id is used and what object is excluded?
Whether the BP+06h parameter is the item instance (receiver) or the viewer/player PID in this engine's calling convention (inference favors receiver but uncertainty remains).

main_function.name: U8P_CANOPYTP::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the 'U8P_CANOPYTP::look' event for a canopy object by producing its short descriptive text via Item::bark and propagating the bark result to the script's outcome.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — the player/actor performs a 'U8P_CANOPYTP::look' (examine) action on the canopy object (entering its interaction/trigger area).

function_map.name: U8P_CANOPYTP::look
function_map.role: Main event handler for the canopy object's 'U8P_CANOPYTP::look' action; orchestrates local state and calls Item::bark to emit the description.

external_calls.purpose: Cause the canopy item to emit/display the short descriptive string "canopy ". The returned value is captured and used (via an 'implies' operation) to set/propagate the script's result or control flow.
